memoryArtifacts.description:
WILLIAM COFFIN OF ALWINGTON was born about 1400 of Alwington, Devon, England, to John Coffin of Alwington (1347-1409) and Thomasin Hathey (1365-1393.)  He married Margaret Cockworthy Gifford.

William …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Giffard於大約1404年出生在英國戴文時，她的父親Thomas Giffard4歲，母親Wilmot Knight1歲。她於1453年與Sir William Coffyn在英國英格蘭戴文結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1486年9月11日在英國英格蘭享年83歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Giffard于大约1404年出生在英国戴文时，她的父亲Thomas Giffard4岁，母亲Wilmot Knight1岁。她于1453年与Sir William Coffyn在英国英格兰戴文结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1486年9月11日在英国英格兰享年83岁。

nameMeanings.name: Giffard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: (i) relationship name from the Continental Germanic personal name Gifard. (ii) nickname, perhaps sometimes from Old French giffard ‘chubby-cheeked, bloated’.

variants.name: Jeffords
meanings: English: variant of Gifford , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Guiver
meanings: English: (i) nickname, apparently from Middle English giver ‘giver, donor, bestower’. (ii) variant of Giffard with voicing of /f/ to /v/ and loss of final -d.

variants.name: Gafford
meanings: English (southeastern): assimilated form of Gatford, a name of various origins. In Sussex and Surrey it is perhaps a variant of the habitational name Catford from a place so called in Somerset. Gatford is also from the habitational names Gateford (in Worksop, Nottinghamshire) or Gateforth (near Selby, Yorkshire), both formed with Old English gāt ‘goat’ + ford ‘ford’, influenced by Old Norse geit ‘goat’.

Probably also an altered form of German Gaffert: habitational name from Gaffert near Köslin, Brandenburg, or from a personal name formed with Middle High German gate ‘fellow, companion’. The surname Gaffert is very rare in Germany.

English: perhaps an altered form of Gatewood , an occupational name for a ‘goatherd’ (Middle English gate-werde, Old English gātweard) or for a ‘gate keeper’ (Middle English gateward, Old English ʒeatweard, dialect gātward).

variants.name: Gifford
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Giffard, either from the Middle English personal name Gifard, derived from an Old French name of this form deriving from ancient Germanic geb- ‘give, gift’ + hard- ‘hardy, brave’, or perhaps sometimes a nickname from Old French gif(f)ard ‘chubby-cheeked, bloated’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Giffard was born about 1404, in Devon, England, her father, Thomas Giffard, was 4 and her mother, Wilmot Knight, was 1. She married Sir William Coffyn in 1453, in Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 11 September 1486, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 83.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Giffard est née environ 1404, à Devon, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Giffard, avait 4 ans et sa mère, Wilmot Knight, avait 1 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William Coffyn en 1453, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 11 septembre 1486, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 83 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Giffard um 1404 in Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Giffard 4 und ihre Mutter Wilmot Knight 1. Sie hat 1453 in Devon, England, United Kingdom Sir William Coffyn geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 11. September 1486 im Alter von 83 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Giffard è nata il circa 1404 nel Devon, England, sua padre, Thomas Giffard, aveva 4 anni e sua madre, Wilmot Knight, aveva 1 anni. Lei sposato Sir William Coffyn nel 1453, nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 11 settembre 1486, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 83 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Giffard(이)가 대략 1404년 영국 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Giffard(은)는 4세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Wilmot Knight(은)는 1세였다. 그녀는 1453년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 Sir William Coffyn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1486년 9월 11일 영국 잉글랜드에서 83세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Giffard nasceu aproximadamente 1404, em Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Giffard, tinha 4 anos e sua mãe, Wilmot Knight, tinha 1 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William Coffyn em 1453, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 11 de setembro de 1486, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 83 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Giffard родилась около 1404 г. в местности девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Giffard было 4 года, а матери, которую звали Wilmot Knight, 1. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Coffyn в 1453 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 11 сентября 1486 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 83 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Giffard nació aproximadamente 1404, en Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Giffard, tenía 4 años y su mamá, Wilmot Knight, tenía 1 años. Se casó con Sir William Coffyn en 1453, en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 11 de septiembre de 1486, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 83 años.
